message:
test(proptest): add property-based tests for reports module pure helpers

Makes four internal helper functions in reports.rs public so they can be
exercised from an external integration test file:

  - format_ts(epoch_ms) -> String: UTC timestamp formatting
  - days_to_ymd(days) -> (year, month, day): Hinnant calendar algorithm
  - format_duration(ms) -> String: tiered human-readable duration
  - truncate(s, max) -> String: bounded string truncation with ellipsis

New file: tests/proptest_reports.rs (492 lines, ~28 properties)

days_to_ymd properties (2000 cases each):
  - Roundtrip via inverse ymd_to_days oracle recovers original day count
  - Month always in [1, 12], day always in [1, 31]
  - Day never exceeds its month's maximum (leap-year aware)
  - Feb 29 only occurs in leap years
  - Monotonicity: consecutive days always advance the date
  - Jan 1 identity for years 1970-2200
  - 400-year leap year cycles (2000, 2400, 1600)
  - Century non-leap exclusion (1900, 2100, 2200, 2300)

format_ts properties (2000 cases each):
  - Always ends with 'Z' (UTC indicator)
  - Contains expected separators (dash, colon, dot, space)
  - Fixed 24-char length for 4-digit year timestamps
  - Millisecond component bounded [0, 999]
  - Hour/minute/second components within valid ranges
  - Month [01-12], day [01-31] in date portion
  - Lexicographic monotonicity (later timestamps >= earlier)
  - Epoch consistency (millis 0-999 all produce 1970-01-01 prefix)

format_duration properties (2000 cases each):
  - Tier 1 (<1000ms): exact numeric "Nms" format
  - Tier 2 (1-60s): ends with 's', parseable as float in [1.0, 60.0]
  - Tier 3 (>=60s): contains 'm' and 's', components parse correctly,
    minutes*60 + seconds == ms/1000
  - Non-empty output for all non-negative inputs
  - Exact tier boundary behavior at 999/1000 and 59999/60000

truncate properties (2000 cases each):
  - Short strings (len <= max) returned unchanged
  - Output byte length bounded by max + 3 (ellipsis UTF-8 width)
  - Ellipsis present iff string was actually truncated
  - Truncated output preserves input prefix
  - Idempotent for already-short strings
  - Empty input always produces empty output
  - max=0 always produces ellipsis for non-empty strings

Cross-function integration (500 cases):
  - format_ts output composable with truncate
  - format_duration output composable with truncate
  - days_to_ymd midnight timestamps consistent with format_ts date prefix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
